title: hydrocotyle rotundifolia var. batrachium
intro: Hydrocotyle rotundifolia var. batrachium, also known as Pennywort or Indian Pennywort, is a perennial aquatic plant native to tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing stress, improving cognitive function, and lowering blood sugar levels. The plant can be consumed as a herbal preparation, often in the form of capsules, tea, or extracts, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
